nodes.label: Direction 3 — forward-only alternatives to SCFF for the deep part
nodes.rationale: The strongest practical direction: keep SCFF up front, use a different forward-only learner deep; forks into unsupervised-local (GIM/CLAPP, on-identity) vs supervised-local (Mono-Forward, safe fallback) — same fork as the rest of Phase 3.

nodes.label: Greedy InfoMax (GIM) / CLAPP family
nodes.rationale: InfoNCE per module, predicts future/neighbouring representations, info-preserving, composes with depth, matches end-to-end CPC — 'SCFF but with a depth-composing objective'; CLAPP is the single-sample Hebbian-plausible version, CLAPP++ matches backprop-SSL on CIFAR-10.

nodes.label: SoftHebb (purest local rule)
nodes.rationale: Unsupervised soft-WTA Hebbian, no targets/errors at all — even cheaper than SCFF (no negative needed) but lower accuracy ceiling and WTA-structured (a different cell than ours).

nodes.label: Mono-Forward (supervised-local, flat-MLP-native)
nodes.rationale: Each layer gets a tiny projection to goodness-as-logits + local cross-entropy, no signal crosses layers; solves depth, beats backprop on MLP CIFAR-10/100; the safe proven win and later becomes the base of Phase-5 deep-supervision readout heads.

nodes.label: The unsupervised-vs-supervised-bulk fork
nodes.rationale: Not 'which algorithm is most accurate' but 'do you keep the bulk unsupervised' — default to GIM/CLAPP (keeps continual win + identity), hold Mono-Forward as the de-risked fallback if the predictive objective fails to compose on flat-MLP vectors.
